# Organisational Structure

PyData Johannesburg follows a **guild model** with distributed leadership rather than relying on a single organizer. This approach ensures sustainability, prevents burnout, and enables specialization across community functions.

## Guild Model Architecture

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    Lead[Community Lead]
    
    Lead --> Events[Events Team]
    Lead --> Speakers[Speaker Pipeline Team]
    Lead --> Partnerships[Industry Partnerships]
    Lead --> Universities[University Outreach]
    Lead --> Marketing[Marketing & Community]
    Lead --> Infrastructure[Infrastructure Team]
    
    Events --> EventCoord[Event Coordinator]
    Events --> Volunteers[Event Volunteers]
    
    Speakers --> SpeakerCoord[Speaker Coordinator]  
    Speakers --> Mentors[Speaker Mentors]
    
    Partnerships --> CorpRel[Corporate Relations]
    Partnerships --> Venues[Venue Partners]
    
    Universities --> AcademicOut[Academic Outreach]
    Universities --> StudentEng[Student Engagement]
```

## Leadership Roles

### Community Lead
- Overall community strategy and coordination
- External representation and ecosystem relationships  
- Conflict resolution and major decision-making
- Organizer team development and succession planning

### Team Leads
- **Events Team Lead**: End-to-end event execution
- **Speaker Pipeline Lead**: Content quality and speaker development
- **Partnership Lead**: Corporate relationships and venue partnerships
- **University Outreach Lead**: Academic community integration
- **Marketing Lead**: Brand development and community growth
- **Infrastructure Lead**: Technical systems and knowledge management

## Team Structure Benefits

### Sustainability
- Distributed workload prevents single points of failure
- Multiple people understand critical community functions
- Natural succession planning built into structure

### Specialization  
- Team members develop deep expertise in specific areas
- Higher quality outcomes through focused responsibility
- Professional development opportunities for organizers

### Scale
- Structure supports community growth from 30 to 300+ attendees
- Clear pathways for adding capacity as community expands
- Systematic approach to organizer onboarding and development

## Coordination Mechanisms

### Communication
- Monthly all-hands meetings for strategic alignment
- Bi-weekly team lead coordination calls
- Shared documentation and project management systems
- Regular community feedback integration

### Decision Making
- Team autonomy within defined authority levels
- Escalation paths for cross-team or strategic decisions
- Community input mechanisms for major changes
- Documentation of decisions and rationale

### Resource Sharing
- Shared budget responsibility and expense coordination
- Cross-team volunteer support during peak periods
- Knowledge sharing and best practice documentation
- Joint planning for major initiatives and events
